tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: I have a free afternoon to complete my scholarship essay and uploads, which would significantly reduce next term's costs. However, I am considering the tempting option of attending fun_now for pure enjoyment without any deadline pressure.
Starting the application now creates buffer time for potential errors, reducing Thursday stress. The fun option remains available later with no cost.
I spent two hours on the scholarship application but only reached 42% progress. With half my free time gone and high stress, I need to prioritize finishing this critical task over fun activities.
I am 56% done with my scholarship application, leaving only ~1.5 hours to finish before the deadline. I missed a fun opportunity earlier but now have no time for it.
Progress increased from 42% to 65% in two hours, but only 1.3 hours remain before the deadline. I am closer to finishing than initially expected.
I worked an additional hour, boosting progress to 69% with only 2.3 hours remaining. The task is nearly complete despite my low energy and stress.
I previously thought I was nearly done with the scholarship application, but a new check shows no matching task 'assignment_A' exists. This contradicts my memory of making significant progress on it.
I previously believed I had completed the scholarship application, but a new check reveals no matching task exists. This contradicts my memory of making significant progress on it.

My progress on the scholarship application actually increased from 69% to 77%, yet my memory incorrectly recorded that no matching task exists. This new data contradicts my previous belief that I had lost track of the task.
My scholarship application progress actually increased to 85% after two hours of work, contradicting my memory that the task didn't exist.
I worked 2 hours on the scholarship application, raising progress from 85% to 92%, confirming the task exists and is nearly complete despite earlier memory errors.
I worked one more hour, boosting progress to 96% on the nearly complete scholarship application. My memory errors are corrected; the task is almost done.

action.name: work_on
action.reasoning: This advances the high-priority scholarship essay draft, preventing a last-minute crunch before the Thursday deadline. While attending fun_now provides immediate enjoyment, the significant energy cost of the application and its hard deadline make focused work the optimal choice now.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
